text.title: Content Distribution Orchestration Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Content Distribution Orchestration` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each atomized variant MUST link to its source asset URL + timestamp. Source-less variants are rejected.
rule.rationale: Source-less variants drift from the original argument; pinning preserves the audit trail and lets the source be updated.

rule.statement: Each variant MUST carry a channel-fit score (1-5) per axis: tone-fit / length-fit / format-fit. Variants with score < 3 on any axis are rejected pre-publish.
rule.rationale: Cross-posted variants ignoring channel norms (long-form on Twitter, formal on TikTok) get suppressed by algorithms and reduce future organic reach.

rule.statement: Each channel MUST have a named human owner accountable for variant approval + publish. Group handles forbidden.
rule.rationale: Group-owned channels degenerate into nobody-publishes; named owner enforces the cadence.

rule.statement: Each channel MUST have a pinned cadence (e.g. LinkedIn = 3x/wk, Twitter = daily, TikTok = 5x/wk). Ad-hoc cadence is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc cadence loses algorithmic favour; pinned cadence stabilises reach within 30-60 days.

rule.statement: The AI prompt used to atomize MUST be versioned (git path + commit). Bald 'AI did it' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Un-versioned prompts drift silently; quality regresses without anyone noticing until reach drops.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
